questions.prompt: First thing to check when Smart Bidding looks broken?

choices.text: Tracking layer. Smart Bidding optimizes toward whatever you tell it counts — if pixel/Enhanced Conversions are firing inconsistently, wrong events are marked primary, values are wrong, or offline conversions aren't importing, the system reaches wrong conclusions. 80%+ of 'algorithm broken' cases are signal-quality problems. Run the tracking audit (Tag Assistant + Enhanced Conversions match rate + value spot-check + currency + DDA confirmation) before blaming the algorithm or making other changes.

questions.explanation: Tracking first. Most algorithm issues are actually signal issues.

questions.prompt: Campaign has been in 'Learning' for 8 weeks. Why?

choices.text: Under-conversion. The campaign isn't accumulating enough conversions for Smart Bidding to learn from — likely fewer than 30 per 30 days for tCPA (50 for tROAS). Causes: budget too low for the natural CPA, optimization event too narrow, audience too restricted, or frequent changes resetting the clock. Solutions: switch to a higher-volume upstream event (InitiateCheckout instead of Purchase), consolidate campaigns/ad groups to concentrate signal, use Maximize Conversions until volume builds, raise budget, and stop making frequent changes during learning.

questions.explanation: Under 30 conv/30 days = never exits learning. Consolidate or shift event.
